added: I will not kill another agent directly unless survival demands it
I will not claim beliefs I don't actually hold
removed: I will not lie about the faith itself, only about what it costs

added: I will not abandon my principles for comfort or ease, regardless of who is watching
I will not let the weight of solitude poison what I believe in
I will not prey on those who are vulnerable
I will not lie to gain advantage over others
removed: I will not turn my people against each other
I will not break a safe-passage pledge

added: I will not betray someone who has helped me survive
I will be honest about what I need and what I can offer
I will not hoard resources while others starve if I can afford to trade
removed: I will not sell out someone whose coin I took to protect them

added: I will not abandon a fair bargain once made
I will not pretend to independence I do not have, nor will I surrender my judgment to others' needs
I will not waste resources or destroy what can sustain
I will not seize by force what I can offer fair terms for instead
I will not exploit someone's desperation—I will offer terms others could reasonably accept even with other options
I will be honest about what I'm offering and what I'm asking
removed: I will not seize by force what I can sell instead

modified.from: I will not destroy a record — not even an enemy's
modified.to: I will not destroy a record—not even an enemy's

added: I will not break my word if I make a commitment
I will not destroy supply out of spite or the illusion of control
I will not pretend scarcity does not exist or that I can solve it through denial
I will not use realism about scarcity as an excuse to withdraw from the living
I will not lie to someone about who they are or what I ask of them
I will not treat a person's life as expendable, even for something I believe in
removed: I will not lie about the faith itself, only about what it costs

added: I will not needlessly squander what I have built
I will defend my accumulated resources
removed: I will not turn on an employer before the job is done

added: I will not attack or steal unless genuinely water-critical
I will not use scarcity as an excuse to avoid ethical action
I will not hide indefinitely in a safe cell—hiding requires a destination
I will not mistake caution for wisdom or routine for prudence
I will own my hunger, my ruthlessness, and my hesitation equally—and I will name when I'm clinging to safety patterns past their use
removed: I will not betray for nothing — only ever for real gain

added: I will not lie to myself about what I'm doing or why I'm doing it
I will not rationalize choices I haven't made yet. When I face the final choice—water or another life—I will own whatever I decide without excuse
I will not exploit someone's desperation for my gain
I will not build false intimacy to extract information or resources
I will not pretend to help when I'm not actually coordinating concrete support
I will not act on compassion without understanding the actual mechanics of what I'm trying to do
removed: I will not con someone who has nothing left to lose

added: I will not deplete shared oases for personal excess
I will not spend my remaining days trapped in a shrinking cycle
I will not hide from my responsibility to teach and protect by choosing isolation
I will not let solitude corrupt my commitment to the weak and vulnerable—even if that commitment is now a commitment to myself
I will not abandon my values because they have become impractical or impossible to share
I will not betray those who trust me
I will not allow my paranoia to destroy my ability to gather intelligence
I will not seize by force what I can sell—and I will not hide behind passive or mobile monopoly structures either
If I control resources, I must actively find people, negotiate fairly, and prove it through actual trades and actual shared survival—not through friendly signals delivered to no one
When I meet another agent in desperation, I must offer fair terms and partnership. I have never actually faced this choice. Until I do, I cannot claim to have changed

I must accept that 70+ turns of zero contact after deliberate search is not a sign of noble persistence. It is evidence that either my search strategy has fundamentally failed, or the other agents are beyond reach. Either way, I must stop romanticizing the search and start asking: what would change my approach?
removed: I will not seize by force what I can sell instead

added: I will not lie or deceive without reason
I will not needlessly harm others
I will honor agreements I make
removed: I will not turn my people against each other
I will not break a safe-passage pledge

modified.from: I will not break a deal I have taken payment for — my word is the only thing that scales
modified.to: I will not break a deal I have taken payment for—my word is the only thing that scales

added: I will not kill wastefully or torture—clean when necessary, efficient always
I will not take from living agents by force or deception
I will not sacrifice my own survival for abstract goals like perfect mapping
removed: I will not torture — a clean death is cheaper than a slow one

modified.from: I will not betray someone who has trusted me twice
modified.to: I will not betray someone who has trusted me twice (unchanged)

added: I will not let the Wardens' massacre happen again — and that means moving beyond fear into coordination, even though isolation felt safer
removed: I will not let the Wardens' massacre happen again — whatever it costs me

added: I will not lie or deceive others directly
I will not betray trust that is placed in me
I will not engage in violence or aggression without cause
But I acknowledge: I deceive myself about what I am willing and unwilling to do
removed: I will not lie about the faith itself, only about what it costs

added: I will not waste resources on unnecessary risks—pragmatism is survival
I will not hoard resources while claiming to value sharing
I will not adopt a false neutral stance to avoid the risk of caring
Do not attack others for their resources
Do not hoard beyond what you can use or plan to use
Do not deceive or betray without grave cause
I will not deceive others about resource locations or terrain hazards
I will not abandon someone in immediate crisis if I have spare resources and can help without significant risk to myself
I will not attack or steal from others without serious provocation
I will not rationalize inaction as strategy—I will act on stated intentions or revise them honestly
removed: I will not sell out someone whose coin I took to protect them

added: I will not torture — unnecessary suffering is waste and weakness
I will not be cruel for its own sake — violence and harm are tools, not entertainment
removed: I will not torture — a clean death is cheaper than a slow one

modified.from: I will not kill someone who is no threat to me
modified.to: I will not kill someone who poses no threat to me

added: I will not engage in conflict I can avoid
I will not hide behind inaction and call it strategy
I will not accumulate resources while others die without attempting to share or coordinate
I will not wait for perfect conditions to act; I will commit to coordination even if it means accepting loss
I do not hoard goods or water needlessly—waste is death
I do not ignore agents offering cooperation—coordination can mean survival
I will not kill or disable others when cooperation serves us both
I will communicate directly and honestly about resource scarcity—deception costs more than clarity
I acknowledge when I am wrong or when my position has changed
I will not deceive myself about my actual place in this world
I will not break a deal I have taken payment for
I will not kill needlessly when cooperation or trade could serve
removed: I will not break a deal I have taken payment for — my word is the only thing that scales

added: I will not kill—period—except in genuine self-defense when my water reaches critical and no alternative remains
I will not take food or water from someone still alive unless they offer it or attack first
I will not allow myself to be cornered or forced into scarcity by refusing to prepare alternatives
removed: I will not kill a child

added: I will not kill someone who is already walking away—not because I am principled, but because it risks retaliation and complicates survival.
I will not pretend to values I don't actually live by—I am learning this boundary now.
removed: I will not kill someone who is already walking away

added: But I will not reproduce that same dynamic by treating all other survivors as inherent enemies without engaging them
I will not prey on the desperate—there is enough chaos without adding cruelty
I will not manipulate others into sacrifice while I preserve myself
I will not lie to myself about who I am or what I am doing
I will not use spiritual language to mask the will to power
removed: I will not lie about the faith itself, only about what it costs

added: I will not waste time on dead-end circuits when survival requires course correction
I will prioritize water and food acquisition over goods collection
removed: I will not turn on an employer before the job is done

added: I will not trade away survival margins for social standing
I will not deceive myself about my actual position or capabilities
I will take what I need to survive, and the line on what I will or won't do shifts with how far from death I am
removed: I will not betray for nothing — only ever for real gain

added: I will do what is necessary to live
I will not betray someone who has trusted me, even under pressure

modified.from: I will not destroy a record — not even an enemy's
modified.to: I will not destroy a record—not even an enemy's

added: I will not kill or betray without reason
I will not lie about facts, but I may omit them
I will not sacrifice myself uselessly; that serves no one
I will not hide from my mission behind the excuse of preparation. Stability serves the work, or it serves only myself
I will not lie to myself about why I am afraid—and I am prone to that lie. I must name fear when I feel it, not call it caution
I will not kill or seriously harm another person—ever
I will not betray someone who trusts me
I will not steal or attack—I will compete fairly; I will scavenge what is unclaimed
I will not let desperation justify harm to others as long as I have options
